{{Quote|I buy tons of things. Anything really. Droid parts, maybe some old weapons you aren't using. I'll take them off your hands.|Anniya, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:TPS" />}}
'''Anniya''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] who lived during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], she resided aboard [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]], in the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Bright Jewel system]]. There, she was working as a [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]], buying [[Droid/Legends|droid]] parts, old weapons and various other things from [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] that passed by. She was one of the two junk dealers working on Tansarii Point Station, along with [[Eocade]], and she could frequently be found on the main level of the station.<ref name="SWG:TPS" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Anniya_old.jpg|thumb|left|100px|A known depiction of Anniya as a Human female]]
Anniya appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Anniya was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]],"<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> In the game, he was a junk dealer that players could sell loot and other items they no longer needed to her.<ref name="SWG:TPS" />

When Tansarii Point Station and its inhabitants was first introduced into the game, Anniya was depicted under a generic spawn template that included at least one known [[Human/Legends|Human]] female depiction. Her appearance was later permanently changed to that of a female Zabrak.
